threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call your bags 'shopping bags' instead of 'tote bags.' The locals will let you know it’s a whole vibe change.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the bus with acoustic covers. Wasn’t half bad, but the driver looked utterly fed up.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using a bus stop as a skate ramp while the buses circled around them. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Späti that doubles as a gallery, showcasing local artists while selling you a late-night beer. Such a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know not to complain about the Muni delays after a dog on the tracks—it's just Tuesday in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning. A guy was giving an impromptu poetry reading to a captivated audience—only in the Tenderloin, I swear.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always ask for 'authentic' tapas but miss the gems tucked away in the alleys; locals load up on pintxos at any hour.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still using their phones for translations at the market—locals just nod and barter in Catalan like it’s second nature.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of 'the underground' or 'the line'—definitely a newbie marker.
Saw a guy on the 59 last night trying to sell homemade jam. Claimed it was 'the only food the late-night crowd needs'.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The way the kebab shop owner remembers your order like it's his secret recipe—definitely a sign you’ve settled in.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a spontaneous dance party on a U-Bahn platform at 3 AM—complete with DJs and a crowd that knows how to vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local by their reaction to the BART delays; a sigh and a shrug, not a panic. It’s just part of the commute here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo pull out a saxophone on the 19. He played for a few stops while we all just sat there, frozen in the moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always queue for entry at Parc Güell, but they miss the real charm at the lesser-known Plaça del Sol, where locals gather over drinks.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still call it ‘Sagrada Familia’ instead of just ‘La Sagrada.’ Plus, their camera is out for every corner. Classic signs.
